decision_target: Define explicit roots, permissions, environment, credentials, and denial behavior
source_of_truth: Direct Claude Node spawn probes, shipped provider code/tests, installed ACP 1.2.1 schema, real Codex ACP smoke, and npm registry versions
read_method: Replay Claude two-turn and cancel probes; inspect Codex ACP new-session, permission, cancel, and cleanup paths; rerun focused ACP integration smoke
max_age: 7 days or any Claude, Codex, codex-acp, or ACP SDK version change
evidence_refs_or_hashes: command:PUMPED_INTEGRATION=1 pnpm --filter @pumped-fn/sdk-codex test -- tests/codex.integration.test.ts -t invokes Codex through ACP
replay_command_or_checker: node .okra/runs/managed-agent-sessions-20260713/drafts/claude-session-probe.mjs && node .okra/runs/managed-agent-sessions-20260713/drafts/claude-cancel-probe.mjs && PUMPED_INTEGRATION=1 pnpm --filter @pumped-fn/sdk-codex test -- tests/codex.integration.test.ts -t 'invokes Codex through ACP'
questions_answered: Claude direct spawn preserves explicit permission mode and one session across two turns
Claude SIGINT produces a structured cancellation result and leaves no live PID
ACP provides cwd, additional roots, permission callbacks, mandatory cancel, and empty MCP configuration
Shipped Codex ACP omits additional roots and does not await child exit
questions_unanswered: Final structured permission-policy type after shared-contract DKR
decision: candidate_accept_explicit_roots_fail_closed_permissions_and_awaited_cleanup; fund isolated codex-acp compatibility PKR after DKR-2
